sentences.sentence: The joke comes over the headphones: 'Which side of a dog has the most hair?
sentences.translation: 耳机里传来一个笑话：“狗的哪一边毛最多？”

structure.text: Which side of a dog has the most hair

difficult_points: come over 表示声音传来。
which side 作疑问主语。

sentences.sentence: The left.' No, not funny.
sentences.translation: “左边。”不，不好笑。
difficult_points: The left 是简短回答。
not funny 省略 it is。

sentences.sentence: Try again.
sentences.translation: 再试一次。
difficult_points: 祈使句省略主语 you。
again 表示重新讲。

sentences.sentence: 'Which side of a dog has the most hair?
sentences.translation: “狗的哪一边毛最多？”
difficult_points: which side of a dog 作疑问主语。
the most hair 是最高级数量。

sentences.sentence: The outside.' Hah!
sentences.translation: “外边。”哈！
difficult_points: 答案利用 inside/outside 的歧义。
Hah 是笑声感叹词。

sentences.sentence: The punchline is silly yet fitting, tempting a smile, even a laugh.
sentences.translation: 包袱既傻气又贴切，令人微笑甚至发笑。

structure.text: tempting a smile, even a laugh

difficult_points: yet 连接相反形容词。
tempting 表示引发的结果。

sentences.sentence: Laughter has always struck people as deeply mysterious, perhaps pointless.
sentences.translation: 笑声一直让人觉得神秘，也许毫无意义。
difficult_points: strike someone as 表示给人某种印象。
perhaps pointless 是补充评价。

sentences.sentence: The writer Arthur Koestler dubbed it the luxury reflex: 'unique in that it serves no apparent biological purpose'.
sentences.translation: 作家Arthur Koestler称之为“奢侈反射”：独特之处在于没有明显生物用途。

structure.text: unique in that it serves no apparent biological purpose

difficult_points: dub A B 表示把A称为B。
in that 引出独特原因。

sentences.sentence: Theories about humour have an ancient pedigree.
sentences.translation: 幽默理论有悠久渊源。
difficult_points: pedigree 比喻思想渊源。
about humour 修饰 theories。

sentences.sentence: Plato expressed the idea that humour is simply a delighted feeling of superiority over others.
sentences.translation: 柏拉图认为幽默只是优越于他人带来的愉悦感。

structure.text: the idea that humour is simply a delighted feeling of superiority over others

difficult_points: that 从句说明 idea 内容。
of superiority over others 修饰 feeling。

sentences.sentence: Kant and Freud felt that joke-telling relies on building up a psychic tension which is safely punctured by the ludicrousness of the punchline.
sentences.translation: 康德和弗洛伊德认为讲笑话依靠积累心理紧张，而包袱的荒诞性会释放这种紧张。

structure.text: that joke-telling relies on building up a psychic tension
which is safely punctured by the ludicrousness of the punchline

difficult_points: rely on 后接动名词。
which 修饰 tension，is punctured 是被动。

sentences.sentence: But most modern humour theorists have settled on some version of Aristotle's belief that jokes are based on a reaction to or resolution of incongruity, when the punchline is either a nonsense or, though appearing silly, has a clever second meaning.
sentences.translation: 现代幽默理论家多接受亚里士多德观点：笑话基于对不协调的反应或解决，包袱要么荒谬，要么虽傻却有巧妙的第二层含义。

structure.text: some version of Aristotle's belief that jokes are based on a reaction to or resolution of incongruity
when the punchline is either a nonsense or, though appearing silly, has a clever second meaning

difficult_points: settle on 表示最终采纳。
either...or... 对比两种包袱形式。

sentences.sentence: Graeme Ritchie, a computational linguist in Edinburgh, studies the linguistic structure of jokes in order to understand not only humour but language understanding and reasoning in machines.
sentences.translation: 爱丁堡计算语言学家Graeme Ritchie研究笑话语言结构，以理解幽默以及机器的语言理解和推理。

structure.text: studies the linguistic structure of jokes
in order to understand not only humour but language understanding and reasoning in machines

difficult_points: in order to 表示目的。
not only...but... 连接研究对象。

sentences.sentence: He says that while there is no single format for jokes, many revolve around a sudden and surprising conceptual shift.
sentences.translation: 他说，笑话虽无单一格式，却常围绕突然的概念转变展开。

structure.text: that while there is no single format for jokes, many revolve around a sudden and surprising conceptual shift
while there is no single format for jokes

difficult_points: while 引导让步从句。
revolve around 表示围绕展开。

sentences.sentence: A comedian will present a situation followed by an unexpected interpretation that is also apt.
sentences.translation: 喜剧演员先呈现情境，再给出意外而恰当的解释。

structure.text: a situation followed by an unexpected interpretation

difficult_points: followed by 修饰 situation。
that 修饰 interpretation。

sentences.sentence: So even if a punchline sounds silly, the listener can see there is a clever semantic fit and that sudden mental 'Aha!' is the buzz that makes us laugh.
sentences.translation: 即使包袱听起来傻，听众仍能看出巧妙的语义契合，那一瞬间的“啊哈！”正是让人发笑的兴奋感。

structure.text: even if a punchline sounds silly
there is a clever semantic fit
that sudden mental 'Aha!' is the buzz that makes us laugh

difficult_points: even if 引导让步。
that makes us laugh 修饰 buzz。

sentences.sentence: Viewed from this angle, humour is just a form of creative insight, a sudden leap to a new perspective.
sentences.translation: 从这个角度看，幽默是一种创造性洞察，是跃入新视角。

structure.text: is just a form of creative insight
a sudden leap to a new perspective

difficult_points: Viewed from... 表示观察角度。
同位语解释 creative insight。

sentences.sentence: However, there is another type of laughter, the laughter of social appeasement and it is important to understand this too.
sentences.translation: 还有另一种笑声，即社会安抚的笑声，理解它也很重要。

structure.text: there is another type of laughter
it is important to understand this too

difficult_points: 同位语解释 another type。
it is important to understand 是形式主语结构。

sentences.sentence: Play is a crucial part of development in most young mammals.
sentences.translation: 玩耍是幼年哺乳动物成长的重要部分。

structure.text: a crucial part of development in most young mammals

difficult_points: in most young mammals 限定 development。
crucial 表示重要。

sentences.sentence: Rats produce ultrasonic squeaks to prevent their scuffles turning nasty.
sentences.translation: 老鼠发出超声声，防止争斗恶化。

structure.text: to prevent their scuffles turning nasty

difficult_points: prevent + 宾语 + 动名词表示防止。
turn nasty 表示恶化。

sentences.sentence: Chimpanzees have a 'play-face' - a gaping expression accompanied by a panting 'ah, ah' noise.
sentences.translation: 黑猩猩有“玩耍脸”——张口表情伴随喘气声。

structure.text: a gaping expression accompanied by a panting 'ah, ah' noise

difficult_points: 同位语解释 play-face。
accompanied by 修饰 expression。

sentences.sentence: In humans, these signals have mutated into smiles and laughs.
sentences.translation: 在人类身上，这些信号演变成微笑和笑声。
difficult_points: mutate into 表示演变成。
现在完成时表示演变结果。

sentences.sentence: Researchers believe social situations, rather than cognitive events such as jokes, trigger these instinctual markers of play or appeasement.
sentences.translation: 研究人员认为，触发这些玩耍或安抚信号的是社会情境，而非笑话等认知事件。

structure.text: social situations, rather than cognitive events such as jokes, trigger these instinctual markers of play or appeasement
rather than cognitive events such as jokes

difficult_points: believe 后接宾语从句；rather than 对比触发因素。
trigger 是从句谓语。

sentences.sentence: People laugh on fairground rides or when tickled to flag a play situation, whether they feel amused or not.
sentences.translation: 人们在游乐设施上或被挠时会笑，以标示玩耍情境，无论是否真的觉得好笑。

structure.text: People laugh on fairground rides or when tickled
whether they feel amused or not

difficult_points: when tickled 省略主语和 be。
whether...or not 表示无论是否。

sentences.sentence: Both social and cognitive types of laughter tap into the same expressive machinery in our brains, the emotion and motor circuits that produce smiles and excited vocalisations.
sentences.translation: 社会性和认知性笑声都调用大脑同一套表达机制，即产生微笑和兴奋发声的情绪与运动回路。

structure.text: Both social and cognitive types of laughter
the same expressive machinery in our brains
the emotion and motor circuits that produce smiles and excited vocalisations

difficult_points: Both...and... 连接两类笑声。
that 修饰 circuits。

sentences.sentence: However, if cognitive laughter is the product of more general thought processes, it should result from more expansive brain activity.
sentences.translation: 如果认知性笑声是普遍思维过程的产物，它应源于更广泛的大脑活动。

structure.text: if cognitive laughter is the product of more general thought processes
it should result from more expansive brain activity

difficult_points: if 提出假设；it 指 cognitive laughter。
result from 表示源于。

sentences.sentence: Psychologist Vinod Goel investigated humour using the new technique of 'single event' functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI).
sentences.translation: 心理学家Vinod Goel用单事件功能性磁共振成像研究幽默。

structure.text: using the new technique of 'single event' functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI)

difficult_points: using 分词说明研究方法。
single event 修饰 imaging。

sentences.sentence: An MRI scanner uses magnetic fields and radio waves to track the changes in oxygenated blood that accompany mental activity.
sentences.translation: MRI扫描仪用磁场和无线电波追踪伴随心理活动的含氧血液变化。

structure.text: uses magnetic fields and radio waves
to track the changes in oxygenated blood

difficult_points: to track 表示目的；that 修饰 changes。
oxygenated blood 指含氧血液。

sentences.sentence: Until recently, MRI scanners needed several minutes of activity and so could not be used to track rapid thought processes such as comprehending a joke.
sentences.translation: 直到最近，MRI仍需数分钟数据，因而无法追踪理解笑话等快速思维过程。

structure.text: MRI scanners needed several minutes of activity
and so could not be used to track rapid thought processes such as comprehending a joke

difficult_points: and so 表示结果；could not be used 是被动。
such as 后列举快速过程。

sentences.sentence: New developments now allow half-second 'snapshots' of all sorts of reasoning and problem-solving activities.
sentences.translation: 新发展可以对各种推理和解决问题活动进行半秒快照。

structure.text: half-second 'snapshots' of all sorts of reasoning and problem-solving activities

difficult_points: allow 后接 snapshots；half-second 作定语。
reasoning 与 problem-solving 并列。

sentences.sentence: Although Goel felt being inside a brain scanner was hardly the ideal place for appreciating a joke, he found evidence that understanding a joke involves a widespread mental shift.
sentences.translation: 虽然Goel认为脑扫描仪内不是欣赏笑话的理想场所，但他发现理解笑话涉及广泛心理转变。

structure.text: Although Goel felt being inside a brain scanner was hardly the ideal place for appreciating a joke
that understanding a joke involves a widespread mental shift

difficult_points: Although 让步；being inside... 是 felt 的宾语从句。
understanding 是 that 从句的动名词主语。

sentences.sentence: His scans showed that at the beginning of a joke the listener's prefrontal cortex lit up, particularly the right prefrontal believed to be critical for problem solving.
sentences.translation: 扫描显示，笑话开头听众前额叶皮层被激活，尤其是被认为对解决问题至关重要的右前额叶。

structure.text: that at the beginning of a joke the listener's prefrontal cortex lit up
particularly the right prefrontal believed to be critical for problem solving

difficult_points: lit up 表示被激活；believed to be 是被动分词。
particularly 突出右侧区域。

sentences.sentence: But there was also activity in the temporal lobes at the side of the head (consistent with attempts to rouse stored knowledge) and in many other brain areas.
sentences.translation: 但头部两侧的颞叶及许多其他脑区也有活动，这与唤起储存知识的尝试一致。

structure.text: there was also activity in the temporal lobes at the side of the head
consistent with attempts to rouse stored knowledge
and in many other brain areas

difficult_points: there was 的主语是 activity。
consistent with 说明活动意义。

sentences.sentence: Then when the punchline arrived, a new area sprang to life - the orbital prefrontal cortex.
sentences.translation: 包袱出现时，一个新区域被激活——眶额前额叶皮层。
difficult_points: spring to life 表示突然活跃。
破折号后指出区域名称。

sentences.sentence: This patch of brain tucked behind the orbits of the eyes is associated with evaluating information.
sentences.translation: 这块位于眼眶后方的脑区与评估信息有关。

structure.text: This patch of brain tucked behind the orbits of the eyes

difficult_points: tucked behind 修饰 brain patch。
be associated with 后接动名词。

sentences.sentence: Making a rapid emotional assessment of the events of the moment is an extremely demanding job for the brain, animal or human.
sentences.translation: 对当下事件作快速情绪评估，是动物或人类大脑的艰巨工作。

structure.text: Making a rapid emotional assessment of the events of the moment
an extremely demanding job for the brain

difficult_points: Making... 是动名词主语。
animal or human 补充说明适用对象。

sentences.sentence: Energy and arousal levels may need to be retuned in the blink of an eye.
sentences.translation: 能量和唤醒水平可能需要瞬间重新调节。

structure.text: in the blink of an eye

difficult_points: need to be retuned 是被动不定式。
习语表示极短时间。

expressions.text: in the blink of an eye

sentences.sentence: These abrupt changes will produce either positive or negative feelings.
sentences.translation: 这些突然变化会产生正面或负面感受。
difficult_points: either...or... 连接两种 feelings。
These 指前句变化。

sentences.sentence: The orbital cortex, the region that becomes active in Goel's experiment, seems the best candidate for the site that feeds such feelings into higher-level thought processes, with its close connections to the brain's sub-cortical arousal apparatus and centres of metabolic control.
sentences.translation: 眶额皮层似乎最可能是把感受输入高级思维的部位，因为它与皮下唤醒装置和代谢控制中枢联系密切。

structure.text: the region that becomes active in Goel's experiment
seems the best candidate for the site
that feeds such feelings into higher-level thought processes
with its close connections to the brain's sub-cortical arousal apparatus and centres of metabolic control

difficult_points: 两个 that 分别修饰 region 和 site。
with 短语补充说明候选依据。

sentences.sentence: All warm-blooded animals make constant tiny adjustments in arousal in response to external events, but humans, who have developed a much more complicated internal life as a result of language, respond emotionally not only to their surroundings, but to their own thoughts.
sentences.translation: 所有温血动物都会根据外部事件微调唤醒水平，但人类不仅对环境，也对自己的思想作出情绪反应。

structure.text: All warm-blooded animals make constant tiny adjustments in arousal in response to external events
but humans, who have developed a much more complicated internal life as a result of language, respond emotionally not only to their surroundings, but to their own thoughts
who have developed a much more complicated internal life as a result of language

difficult_points: who 修饰 humans；not only...but... 连接反应对象。
in response to 表示对外部事件反应。

sentences.sentence: Whenever a sought-for answer snaps into place, there is a shudder of pleased recognition.
sentences.translation: 每当寻求的答案突然想通，便会产生欣喜的认知震颤。

structure.text: Whenever a sought-for answer snaps into place
there is a shudder of pleased recognition

difficult_points: Whenever 表示每当；snap into place 表示突然想通。
there is 的主语是 shudder。

sentences.sentence: Creative discovery being pleasurable, humans have learned to find ways of milking this natural response.
sentences.translation: 由于创造性发现令人愉悦，人类学会充分利用这种自然反应。

structure.text: have learned to find ways of milking this natural response

difficult_points: 独立主格表示原因。
milk 在此是充分利用的比喻。

sentences.sentence: The fact that jokes tap into our general evaluative machinery explains why the line between funny and disgusting, or funny and frightening, can be so fine.
sentences.translation: 笑话调用一般评估机制这一事实，解释了好笑与恶心或恐怖之间的界线为何如此细微。

structure.text: The fact that jokes tap into our general evaluative machinery
why the line between funny and disgusting, or funny and frightening, can be so fine

difficult_points: fact 后 that 从句说明事实；why 从句是解释对象。
between 短语并列两种界线。

sentences.sentence: Whether a joke gives pleasure or pain depends on a person's outlook.
sentences.translation: 笑话带来愉悦还是痛苦取决于人的观念。

structure.text: Whether a joke gives pleasure or pain

difficult_points: Whether...or... 作主语从句。
depend on 表示取决于。

sentences.sentence: Humour may be a luxury, but the mechanism behind it is no evolutionary accident.
sentences.translation: 幽默或许是奢侈品，但其机制绝非进化偶然。

structure.text: but the mechanism behind it is no evolutionary accident

difficult_points: but 对比表面奢侈和进化必然。
behind it 修饰 mechanism。

sentences.sentence: As Peter Derks, a psychologist at William and Mary College in Virginia, says: 'I like to think of humour as the distorted mirror of the mind.
sentences.translation: 正如心理学家Peter Derks所说：“我喜欢把幽默看作心灵的扭曲镜子。

structure.text: As Peter Derks, a psychologist at William and Mary College in Virginia, says
I like to think of humour as the distorted mirror of the mind

difficult_points: As...says 引出引语。
think of A as B 表示把A视为B。

sentences.sentence: It's creative, perceptual, analytical and lingual.
sentences.translation: 它具有创造性、感知性、分析性和语言性。
difficult_points: 四个形容词并列作表语。
It 指 humour。

sentences.sentence: If we can figure out how the mind processes humour, then we'll have a pretty good handle on how it works in general.'
sentences.translation: 如果能弄清心灵如何处理幽默，我们就能很好理解心灵总体如何运作。”

structure.text: If we can figure out how the mind processes humour
then we'll have a pretty good handle on how it works in general

difficult_points: if...then... 表示条件结果。
have a handle on 表示掌握理解。
